## DOT PLOT chart, SENTINEL style

Render as SVG (or equivalent) on a 960x540 px canvas.
Every position, size, and color below is exact. No adjectives, no improvisation.

### AI metadata (for agent routing)
```json
{
  "code": "CH-DST-02",
  "ovp_version": "1.0",
  "intent": [
    "comparison",
    "distribution"
  ],
  "form": "two dots per category (two measures) on one scale",
  "data_required": "categories with two comparable measures (planned vs actual, min vs max)",
  "family": "DST",
  "alternatives": [
    "CH-DEV-02 (change over time reading)"
  ],
  "see_instead": [
    {
      "when": "the two values are before/after",
      "use": "CH-DEV-02"
    }
  ]
}
```

Use when:
- two measures per category on one scale: planned vs actual, this vs that
- Cleveland dot plot use cases

Do NOT use when:
- before/after change (CH-DEV-02 reads direction)
- one measure (CH-RNK-05)

```json
  "unit": "on first label",
  "series_labels": "[name_a, name_b] labeled on the first row",
  "categories": "array of {label, values: [a, b], highlight?}"
}
```

### Rules
- series identified by direct labels on the first row, never a legend
- dot_a muted, dot_b primary, connector hairline
- one highlight maximum

### Never do this
- legends
- different scales per row
- more than two dots per row

### QA before delivering
- [ ] series labeled on first row
- [ ] highlight <= 1
- [ ] unit once
- [ ] source present

| Element | Color |

| background | `#101418` |
| dot_a | `#5C6B78` |
| dot_b | `#F0B429` |
| connector | `#2A323A` |
| category_label | `#9FB0BE` |
| value_label | `#E6EDF3` |
| title | `#E6EDF3` |
| source | `#9FB0BE` |

### Fonts

- Title font: 'Segoe UI',Arial,sans-serif, weight 600
- All other text: 'Segoe UI',Arial,sans-serif, weight 400 (600 on highlighted items)

### Language chrome

No kicker, no eyebrow; title at the default y.
